extraction.one_line_summary: DUMOULIN Tom dominated the 39.8km rolling time trial at Montefalco, taking pink jersey with 49-second margin over THOMAS Geraint.
tactical_patterns: THOMAS Geraint unexpectedly challenged pure time trialists, going 49s faster than QUINTANA Nairo, revealing GC vulnerability in sustained power efforts.
KIRYIENKA Vasil crashed at predictable technical corner after downhill dip, suggesting recon failure on key descent transition zones.
JUNGELS Bob's second-place finish despite weaker specialization shows medium-mountain TT course rewards diesel engines over pure aerodynamics.

rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: positive: Nearly caught three-minute man and went sub-51 minutes, showing dominance that extends 2:23 GC lead over Quintana.
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: 34-minute split was fastest until Dumoulin, revealing time trial form that threatens GC riders on rolling TT courses.
rider_signals.JUNGELS Bob: positive: Second quickest time signals all-rounder capability beyond climbing reputation, valuable for medium-length time trials.
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: 49-second deficit to Thomas on rolling terrain confirms chronic weakness in sustained threshold efforts outside mountains.
rider_signals.KIRYIENKA Vasil: negative: Crashed at technical corner, compromising Sky's GC support depth at critical race juncture.
